title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence · Inference Brief: Release Evidence
summary: Migrate and publish a production-shaped TypeScript 7 library with measurable compiler performance and consumer proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts release evidence increment in a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify clean install, strict build, tests, declarations, package exports, runtime smoke use, audit, rollback, and consumer acceptance.
Design minimum expressive public types, declarations, errors, and compatibility policy around user tasks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Release Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 4.
